text: The surgeon completes the wedge resection and proceeds with the right upper lobe lobectomy. A Cavidian Tan stapler is used to staple the right upper lobe vein and the truncal branch of the pulmonary artery. Dissection of the subcarinal station seven lymph node is performed, followed by removal of the station R-ten lymph node located in front of the pulmonary artery and below the azygos vein. A sling is placed around the right upper lobe bronchus, and a curve tip stapler is used for the final firing. The posterior ascending branch of the pulmonary artery is visible. The surgeon completes the arterial and bronchial firings and moves to the fissure between the upper and middle lobe, completing the firings from posteriorly to anteriorly. The lobe is placed into a bag and removed.

The surgeon uses a jiggly saw with robotic arms to cut through the third rib posteriorly, followed by the second rib. An axillary incision is utilized to cut the ribs anteriorly with a rib cutter. The ribs are then dissected off the muscles of the anterior chest wall, with assistance pushing the ribs to facilitate cutting in the correct plane.

The surgeon completes the dissection of the second and third ribs positioned under the scapula, using robotic ports and an auxiliary incision. The external view shows the placement of these ports and incision.
